preamble: The transformation of the voiceless alveolar affricate [ts] into the voiced alveolar fricative [z] is a regular phonetic shift, primarily driven by processes of lenition and voicing. This involves the weakening of the affricate's stop component and the acquisition of voicing, often in specific phonetic environments.

examples.note: Syllable-final shift from Proto-Athabascan to Carrier, contrasting with Babine which retained [ts].
The name 'Rotse' became 'Lozi' due to regular phonetic changes, including [ts] to [z].

sources: Poser, W. J. (2020). The Carrier Language: A Brief Introduction. College of New Caledonia.
